title: <div> * Lead-Safe Certification (NJ): We know the inspection cadence is every th
content:
* Lead-Safe Certification (NJ): We know the inspection cadence is every three years or upon tenant turnover (whichever is earlier). We also know the specific distinction between "Lead-Safe" (2-year validity) and "Lead-Free" (permanent) certifications.
2. You have a clear view of the "Competitor Gap"
The research identifies exactly what the incumbents are missing or where they are vulnerable:
* RealPage & Yardi: They are currently under intense scrutiny for antitrust violations regarding their pricing algorithms. This creates a specific opening for a "Compliance-First" pricing tool that explicitly markets itself as not using shared private data.
* Daisy: This competitor is already using AI to automate board governance and compliance for condos/co-ops in NY. This suggests the "Condo/Co-op" niche is active, but the rental landlord compliance niche (Good Cause/Lead Paint) remains less crowded.
* Zillow/StreetEasy: The research confirms that these platforms are focusing on "listing access standards" and limiting "pocket listings," which frustrates agents who want more control. This validates the opportunity for an "Off-Market" network tool.
